task.title: Configuring a WireGuard server by using nmcli

li.p: You have generated the public and private key for both the server and client.
You know the following information:
The private key of the server
The static tunnel IP addresses and subnet masks of the client
The public key of the client
The static tunnel IP addresses and subnet masks of the server

context.p: You can configure the WireGuard server by creating a connection profile in NetworkManager. Use this method to let NetworkManager manage the WireGuard connection.
This procedure assumes the following settings:

li.p: Server:
Private key: YFAnE0psgIdiAF7XR4abxiwVRnlMfeltxu10s/c4JXg=
Tunnel IPv4 address: 192.0.2.1/24
Tunnel IPv6 address: 2001:db8:1::1/32
Client:
Public key: bnwfQcC8/g2i4vvEqcRUM2e6Hi3Nskk6G9t4r26nFVM=
Tunnel IPv4 address: 192.0.2.2/24
Tunnel IPv6 address: 2001:db8:1::2/32

step.cmd: Add a NetworkManager WireGuard connection profile:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection add type wireguard con-name server-wg0 ifname wg0 autoconnect no
info.p: This command creates a profile named server-wg0 and assigns the virtual interface wg0 to it. To prevent the connection from starting automatically after you add it without finalizing the configuration, disable the autoconnect parameter.

step.cmd: Set the tunnel IPv4 address and subnet mask of the server:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify server-wg0 ipv4.method manual ipv4.addresses 192.0.2.1/24

step.cmd: Set the tunnel IPv6 address and subnet mask of the server:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify server-wg0 ipv6.method manual ipv6.addresses 2001:db8:1::1/32

step.cmd: Add the server’s private key to the connection profile:
Set the port for incoming WireGuard connections:

info.p: Always set a fixed port number on hosts that receive incoming WireGuard connections. If you do not set a port, WireGuard uses a random free port each time you activate the wg0 interface.

step.cmd: Add peer configurations for each client that you want to allow to communicate with this server. You must add these settings manually, because the nmcli utility does not support setting the corresponding connection properties.

li.p: Edit the /etc/NetworkManager/system-connections/server-wg0.nmconnection file, and append:
The [wireguard-peer.<public_key_of_the_client>] entry defines the peer section of the client, and the section name contains the public key of the client.
The allowed-ips parameter sets the tunnel IP addresses of the client that are allowed to send data to this server.
Add a section for each client.
Reload the server-wg0 connection profile:

step.cmd: Optional: Configure the connection to start automatically, enter:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify server-wg0 autoconnect yes

step.cmd: If you use this host in a network with DHCP or Stateless Address Autoconfiguration (SLAAC), the connection can be vulnerable to being redirected. For details and mitigation steps, see Assigning a VPN connection to a dedicated routing table to prevent the connection from bypassing the tunnel.

li.p: Display the interface configuration of the wg0 device:
li.codeblock:
# wg show wg0 interface: wg0 public key: UtjqCJ57DeAscYKRfp7cFGiQqdONRn69u249Fa4O6BE= private key: (hidden) listening port: 51820
peer: bnwfQcC8/g2i4vvEqcRUM2e6Hi3Nskk6G9t4r26nFVM= allowed ips: 192.0.2.2/32, 2001:db8:1::2/128
li.p: To display the private key in the output, use the WG_HIDE_KEYS=never wg show wg0 command.
Display the IP configuration of the wg0 device:
li.codeblock: # ip address show wg0 20: wg0: <POINTOPOINT,NOARP,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1420 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000 link/none inet 192.0.2.1/24 brd 192.0.2.255 scope global noprefixroute wg0 valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever inet6 2001:db8:1::1/32 scope global noprefixroute valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever inet6 fe80::3ef:8863:1ce2:844/64 scope link noprefixroute valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

li.p: Configure the firewalld service on the WireGuard server.
